summary: Monte Carlo simulation re-derived with SNR-aware methodology. Key findings: (1) At physically realistic G5-storm SNR (5-100), expected r = 0.961-0.990, making dome r=0.992 entirely unremarkable. (2) The old range r=0.44-0.69 required SNR<0.8 (noise dominates signal), physically impossible for one of the most extreme geomagnetic storms ever recorded. (3) A dome defender could have flipped the old argument: "your simulation says 0.44-0.69, we observe 0.992, the excess proves kappa." The corrected simulation eliminates this vulnerability. Also fixes kappa=1.672->1.677 (10.9/6.5=1.6769). Expansion file contains exact replacement text for detail_evidence Monte Carlo paragraph, finding, claim, and tldr_evidence.
